title: Swarm Art in the Browser with boids4R
text: article shows ggWebGL can render swarm art browser renderer-neutral boids4R simulations. visual idea generative rather statistical: compact schools, predator avoidance, obstacle corridors, murmurations, mixed-species flocks become animated point/vector timelines can panned, zoomed, scrubbed, inspected WebGL. examples deliberately browser-native. boids4R computes flocking frames, ggWebGL turns frames animated primitives shader camera controls. handoff can used gallery pieces, teaching demonstrations, stress tests timeline rendering. Code examples shown default. Live WebGL widgets disabled CRAN, package checks, CI. Rich local pkgdown rendering requires GGWEBGL_EVAL_COVERAGE_VIGNETTE=true GGWEBGL_EVAL_LIVE_WIDGETS=true.

title: Swarm Art in the Browser with boids4R
text: examples mirror boids4R scenario gallery: compact schools, obstacle corridors, predator avoidance, murmurations, mixed-species 3D flocks. widget built renderer-neutral boids_simulation object animated ggWebGL timeline. Colours encode species roles, current boids emphasized, arrows show current velocity, obstacles drawn rings. Optional trails faint recent history window, current boid count.
code: if (!boids4r_available) {   cat("Scenario gallery widgets skipped.\n") } else {   scenario_gallery <- list(     schooling_2d = boids4R::boids_scenario(       "schooling_2d",       n = 180L,       steps = 240L,       record_every = 3L,       seed = 111L     ),     obstacle_corridor_2d = boids4R::boids_scenario(       "obstacle_corridor_2d",       n = 160L,       steps = 240L,       record_every = 3L,       seed = 112L     ),     predator_avoidance_2d = boids4R::boids_scenario(       "predator_avoidance_2d",       n = 170L,       steps = 240L,       record_every = 3L,       seed = 113L     ),     murmuration_3d = boids4R::boids_scenario(       "murmuration_3d",       n = 240L,       steps = 240L,       record_every = 4L,       seed = 114L     ),     mixed_species_3d = boids4R::boids_scenario(       "mixed_species_3d",       n = 210L,       steps = 240L,       record_every = 4L,       seed = 115L     )   )    scenario_widgets <- lapply(names(scenario_gallery), function(name) {     sim <- scenario_gallery[[name]]     spec <- ggWebGL:::ggwebgl_boids_display_spec(       sim,       boid_size = if (identical(sim$dimension, "3d")) 4.0 else 4.2,       prey_size = 4.8,       predator_size = 7.5,       current_alpha = 0.95,       trail_alpha = 0.18,       vector_mode = "current",       vector_every = 1L,       vector_scale = if (identical(sim$dimension, "3d")) 0.11 else 0.13,       obstacle_mode = "ring",       obstacle_segments = 48L,       trail = "recent",       trail_length = 32L,       shader = "default",       speed = 1.4,       fps = 24L     )     spec$labels$title <- paste("boids4R", name)     ggWebGL::ggWebGL(spec, height = if (identical(sim$dimension, "3d")) 540 else 500)   })   names(scenario_widgets) <- names(scenario_gallery) }

title: Swarm Art in the Browser with boids4R
text: custom workflow builds corridor low-level boids4R constructors, compares baseline run stronger obstacle/predator avoidance run.
code: if (!boids4r_available) {   cat("Custom workflow widgets skipped.\n") } else {   bounds <- matrix(     c(-2.4, -1.35, 2.4, 1.35),     ncol = 2,     dimnames = list(c("x", "y"), c("min", "max"))   )    n_school <- 96L   n_scout <- 32L   n_boids <- n_school + n_scout   school_axis <- seq(0, 1, length.out = n_school)   scout_axis <- seq(0, 1, length.out = n_scout)   positions <- rbind(     cbind(-2.18 + 0.83 * school_axis, -0.70 + 0.95 * abs(sin(pi * school_axis))),     cbind(-2.22 + 0.77 * scout_axis, 0.28 + 0.64 * abs(cos(pi * scout_axis)))   )   velocity_phase <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n_boids)   velocities <- cbind(     0.35 + 0.20 * cos(velocity_phase),     0.08 * sin(velocity_phase)   )    custom_state <- boids4R::boids_state(     n_boids,     "2d",     bounds = bounds,     positions = positions,     velocities = velocities,     species = c(rep("school", n_school), rep("scout", n_scout))   )    custom_world <- boids4R::boids_world(     "2d",     bounds = bounds,     boundary = "reflect",     obstacles = data.frame(       x = c(-0.82, -0.05, 0.72),       y = c(0.42, -0.36, 0.48),       radius = c(0.30, 0.36, 0.31)     ),     predators = data.frame(       x = -0.25,       y = 0.92,       radius = 0.58,       strength = 1.2     ),     attractors = data.frame(       x = 2.08,       y = -0.86,       strength = 0.95     )   )    baseline_params <- boids4R::boids_params(     "2d",     separation_weight = 1.35,     alignment_weight = 0.94,     cohesion_weight = 0.62,     obstacle_weight = 2.5,     predator_weight = 2.3,     goal_weight = 0.16,     max_speed = 1.18,     max_force = 0.12,     noise = 0.001   )    avoidance_params <- boids4R::boids_params(     "2d",     separation_weight = 1.35,     alignment_weight = 0.94,     cohesion_weight = 0.62,     obstacle_weight = 2.8,     predator_weight = 3.2,     goal_weight = 0.20,     max_speed = 1.18,     max_force = 0.12,     noise = 0.001   )    custom_runs <- list(     baseline = boids4R::simulate_boids(       custom_state,       custom_world,       baseline_params,       steps = 240L,       record_every = 3L,       seed = 221L     ),     stronger_avoidance = boids4R::simulate_boids(       custom_state,       custom_world,       avoidance_params,       steps = 240L,       record_every = 3L,       seed = 222L     )   )    custom_widgets <- lapply(names(custom_runs), function(name) {     spec <- ggWebGL:::ggwebgl_boids_display_spec(       custom_runs[[name]],       boid_size = 4.4,       prey_size = 5.0,       predator_size = 8.0,       current_alpha = 0.96,       trail_alpha = 0.18,       vector_mode = "current",       vector_every = 1L,       vector_scale = 0.14,       obstacle_mode = "ring",       obstacle_segments = 48L,       trail = "recent",       trail_length = 32L,       shader = "default",       speed = 1.5,       fps = 24L     )     spec$labels$title <- paste("boids4R custom corridor:", name)     ggWebGL::ggWebGL(spec, height = 500)   })   names(custom_widgets) <- names(custom_runs) }

title: Compose a Publication Figure from ggWebGL Panels — compose_ggwebgl_figure
text: panels list panel sources. element may ggplot, ggWebGL widget, ggwebgl_spec, raw renderer payload, image path, list source plus optional wait_seconds, show_panel_overlay, preset overrides. file Output file path. width, height Output size pixels. format Optional image format. omitted, inferred file. dpi Output density metadata used writing image. background Background colour used final flattened image. layout One "single", "row", "grid". labels Optional character vector panel labels drawn top-left corner occupied panel cell. inset Optional list panel source, fractional left, top, width, height, optional border, border_colour, border_alpha. annotations Optional list text annotations. entry contain text plus fractional x y, optional size, colour, font, hjust, vjust. preset Export preset. "publication" adds subtle panel borders muted label styling. selfcontained Passed htmlwidgets::saveWidget() temporary widget captures. wait_seconds Default render delay capture. nrow, ncol Optional grid dimensions used layout = "grid". elementId Optional DOM element id passed panel sources must first turned widgets.

title: Compose a Publication Figure from ggWebGL Panels — compose_ggwebgl_figure
code: old <- options(ggwebgl.reset_processx_supervisor = TRUE) on.exit(options(old), add = TRUE)  point_spec <- ggwebgl_spec(   layers = list(     ggwebgl_layer_points(       data.frame(x = c(0.15, 0.48, 0.82), y = c(0.22, 0.76, 0.38)),       x = "x",       y = "y",       colour = c("#0f766e", "#f97316", "#2563eb"),       alpha = 0.8,       size = 5     )   ),   webgl = list(shader = "default", interactions = character()) ) line_spec <- ggwebgl_spec(   layers = list(     ggwebgl_layer_lines(       data.frame(x = c(0.1, 0.45, 0.8), y = c(0.25, 0.75, 0.35)),       x = "x",       y = "y",       colour = "#334155",       alpha = 0.9,       width = 2     )   ),   webgl = list(shader = "default", interactions = character()) )  out <- tempfile(fileext = ".jpg") compose_ggwebgl_figure(   panels = list(     point_spec,     line_spec   ),   file = out,   layout = "row",   labels = c("points", "lines"),   width = 480,   height = 240,   format = "jpeg",   preset = "clean",   wait_seconds = 0.25 ) file.exists(out) #> [1] TRUE

title: Build a Linked Magnifying-Glass Zoom Scene — ggwebgl_magnify_region
text: source ggplot, ggWebGL widget, ggwebgl_spec, raw renderer payload accepted ggWebGL(). region Rectangle magnify. Use either list(x = c(xmin, xmax), y = c(ymin, ymax)) list(xmin = ..., xmax = ..., ymin = ..., ymax = ...). display One "panel" "inset". source_panel Optional panel id magnify source multiple panels. Defaults first panel. zoom_layers Optional renderer-ready layers use zoom view. omitted, source panel layers reused. global_panel_id, zoom_panel_id Panel ids used display = "panel". global_label, zoom_label Optional panel labels. box Whether add rectangle overlay global panel. box_colour, box_alpha, box_width Rectangle styling. inset Inset placement list display = "inset" fractional left, top, width, height. interactive Whether two-panel magnifier let browser-side brush rectangles global panel update zoom panel viewport live. width, height Optional publication figure dimensions inset output. background, preset Publication figure styling inset output. labels Optional labels derived renderer specs. webgl Optional renderer options derived specs. Defaults source webgl options.

what: Add WebGL Rendering Options to a ggplot — theme_webgl
title: Add WebGL Rendering Options to a ggplot — theme_webgl
text: Attach WebGL-specific rendering metadata ggplot object. returned object consumed ggplot_webgl() stored plot plot$ggwebgl.

title: Add WebGL Rendering Options to a ggplot — theme_webgl
code: theme_webgl(   shader = "default",   antialias = TRUE,   transparent = TRUE,   buffer_size = 65536L,   interactions = c("pan", "zoom"),   interactions_spec = NULL,   rendering = "visualization",   panel_overlay = "auto",   view = NULL,   selection = NULL,   dimension = "2d",   camera = "orbit",   projection = "orthographic",   camera_state = list(),   depth_test = NULL,   blend_mode = "auto",   timeline = NULL,   transport = NULL,   ... )

title: Add WebGL Rendering Options to a ggplot — theme_webgl
text: shader Shader preset name path identifier. Built-modes include point shaders ("default", "density_splat", "uncertainty_alpha", "point_sprite_glow"), trajectory shaders ("trajectory_age", "trajectory_age_glow", "trajectory_velocity", "trajectory_direction"), raster shaders ("raster_texture", "raster_threshold", "raster_contour_overlay"). Mesh surface layers usually select shader material/shading arguments. antialias Logical scalar; whether antialiasing requested. transparent Logical scalar; whether drawing surface allow transparency. buffer_size Integer scalar giving initial buffer allocation used eventual renderer. interactions Legacy character vector interaction modes enable. New code use selection = ggwebgl_selection(...) brush/lasso behavior. interactions_spec Optional ggwebgl_interactions() object. preferred structured interaction contract hover, click, brush, lasso, camera, Shiny event behavior. rendering Rendering contract mode. "visualization" keeps current interactive widget chrome. "publication" suppresses presentation chrome default intended clean figure capture. panel_overlay Panel overlay display mode. "auto" shows panel strips frames faceted plots, "show" forces , "hide" removes . view Optional ggwebgl_view() object. preferred structured view/camera contract. selection Optional ggwebgl_selection() object. preferred selection contract. dimension, camera, projection, camera_state Legacy view fields retained internal migration shim. depth_test Logical scalar. NULL uses renderer default: disabled 2D scenes enabled 3D scenes. Set explicitly override. blend_mode Primitive blending mode: "auto", "alpha", "additive", "premultiplied". timeline Optional ggwebgl_timeline() specification runtime playback controls. transport Optional ggwebgl_transport() object controlling compact typed-array payloads, LOD previews, progressive upload large point layers. ... Reserved future backend-specific options.

title: Build ggWebGL Renderer Options — webgl_spec
text: camera Camera/controller mode. "panzoom" targets 2D scenes; "orbit" "trackball" target 3D scenes. projection Projection mode, "orthographic" "perspective". dimension Optional renderer dimensionality. omitted, "orbit" "trackball" imply "3d" "panzoom" implies "2d". depth_test Logical scalar. NULL enables depth testing 3D scenes disables 2D scenes. blend_mode Primitive blending mode: "auto", "alpha", "additive", "premultiplied". shader Optional shader preset. interactions Optional interaction mode vector. view Optional ggwebgl_view() object. supplied, takes precedence camera, projection, dimension. selection Optional ggwebgl_selection() object. timeline Optional ggwebgl_timeline() object. transport Optional ggwebgl_transport() object. ... Additional renderer options stored webgl$extra.

title: ggWebGL 0.8.0
text: Guarded widgets CRAN CI

title: ggWebGL 0.7.0
text: Added ggwebgl_interactions() structured interaction contract hover, click, brush/lasso, camera, Shiny event behavior. Added renderer-owned hover/click picking coverage additional primitive types, including vectors, raster cells, structured surfaces. Unified Shiny interaction event emission hover, selection, brush, camera, timeline/time state preserving existing timeline update APIs. Added CRAN-safe Shiny interaction demo exercises hover, click, brush, camera, time events without custom JavaScript. Added compact point transport deterministic LOD metadata large point scenes, plus manual dense-scene smoke helpers record payload browser diagnostics without making fixed performance statements. Added neutral scene-type benchmark helpers public examples dense embeddings, trajectory timelines, surface/mesh rendering, Shiny interaction, static-versus-WebGL workflow comparison. Added surface/mesh interactive benchmark vignettes built small deterministic examples manual metric schemas.

title: ggWebGL 0.6.0
text: Added XGeoRTR boids4R Suggests optional integrations keeping hard dependencies. Restored richer boids4R animation swarm-art vignette plus matching htmlwidget example, using installed suggested packages instead sibling-repository loading. Restored live XGeoRTR bridge vignette behavior neutral renderer/backend ownership wording guarded example code. Kept shapViz3D guarded optional ecosystem example without adding package dependencies. Updated dependency bridge tests allow XGeoRTR boids4R Suggests continuing reject hard dependencies optional ecosystem packages.

title: ggWebGL 0.5.0
text: Froze experimental renderer API around structured ggwebgl_view(), ggwebgl_selection(), ggwebgl_material() contracts. Extended vector layers support 3D z/zend coordinates preserving renderer-owned ids, frame/time metadata, browser selection behavior. Added distinct orbit trackball camera control paths normalized rotation-based camera state. Upgraded mesh surface payloads generated normals, Lambert material metadata, picking ids, wireframe/culling controls, GPU payload caching hooks. Expanded interaction-frame benchmark artifact schema fixed frame-rate performance statements require device, browser, GPU, commit, primitive-count, artifact metadata.

title: ggWebGL 0.3.0
text: Added exported ggwebgl_magnify_region() R/magnify-region.R, panel inset modes driven exact data-coordinate rectangle. Added experimental vector-arrow rendering geom_vector_webgl() ggwebgl_layer_vectors(), using renderer-ready shafts triangle arrowheads without backend-specific semantics. Added experimental brush lasso interaction modes emit selected primitive ids Shiny inputs optional JavaScript callbacks. Added experimental timeline controls frame/time filtering primitive layers plus play, scrub, speed, reset UI controls. Added experimental opt-3D camera state, 3D point/line support, indexed mesh payloads, geom_mesh_webgl(), geom_surface_webgl(), ggwebgl_layer_mesh(), ggwebgl_layer_surface(). Added renderer-generic future-work gallery contract tests covering new vector, selection, timeline, camera, mesh, surface paths. Added interaction-frame benchmark schema frame-rate statements. Fixed performance numbers remain future-facing generated benchmark artifacts exist named target machine.

title: ggWebGL 0.2.0
text: Promoted package long-running development scaffold 0.2.0 release line. Added large-scene demo support: persistent point buffers stable large point-cloud interaction, deterministic count-aware density splats, generic hover labels point primitives, reproducible renderer gallery. Added generic downstream adapter helpers renderer-ready point, line, raster primitive specs, including exported ggwebgl_layer_*() constructors ggwebgl_spec(). Added package-owned static export surfaces snapshot_ggwebgl() compose_ggwebgl_figure(), publication-style PNG/JPEG captures simple figure composition can driven package API instead ad hoc scripts. Added project evidence documentation machine-readable evidence summaries internal release validation. Added vignette-local figure copies website articles need images without referencing files outside pkgdown’s allowed article asset paths. Tightened browser-backed static export path around dedicated chromote browser ownership, lighter examples, explicit optional dependency declarations export vignette tooling. Fixed pkgdown/htmlwidget layout containment restoring widget CSS dependency adding inline canvas-stage safeguards, preventing WebGL canvases stacking top article pages.

title: ggWebGL 0.1.0
text: Expanded core renderer beyond initial scaffold reusable showcase helpers, manual htmlwidget Shiny smoke tests, stronger pkgdown-facing documentation surface. Implemented stabilized main point shader modes: default, density_splat, trajectory_age, trajectory_age_glow. Added hover-based sample inspection top existing pan zoom interactions. Added texture-backed raster rendering geom_raster_webgl(). Added fixed-scale facet_wrap() facet_grid() support panel-aware render specification panel-local interaction state. Added packaged real-data evidence examples covering raster fields, storm trajectories, dense embeddings. Added reproducible evaluation suite ggplot2 plotly, including machine-readable metrics benchmark figure scripts inst/benchmarks/. Froze XGeoRTR/ggWebGL renderer boundary RENDERER_CONTRACT.md added first as_ggwebgl_spec.xgeo_state() adapter plus renderer contract tests.

title: ggWebGL 0.0.0.9000
text: Reset package metadata top-level documentation copied scaffold ggWebGL package identity. Rewrote package README, citation, pkgdown configuration around intended ggplot2 plus WebGL rendering workflow companion-package split. Removed imported carryover benchmark content copied package tree. Added first real ggplot_webgl() renderer path point line layers, backed minimal WebGL htmlwidget. Added initial showcase vignette gallery, including dense latent-space, diffusion-trajectory, phase-portrait, optimization-path examples. Added reusable standard high_detail showcase presets gallery demo surfaces.
